items.title: Cockroach leg found in food: Penang Health Dept issues corrective orders
items.summary: Batu Uban assemblyman A. Kumaresan had raised concern over food hygiene enforcement after a cockroach leg was reportedly found in food served at the well-known eatery. Gooi said corrective instructions had been issued to the restaurant operator to ensure full compliance with the Food Act 1983 and the Food Hygiene Regulations 2009. The issue came to light after Kumaresan raised concerns over food hygiene enforcement, warning that neglecting cleanliness and food safety could lead to food poisoning.

items.title: Indians panic-buying fuel despite government assurances
items.summary: AHMEDABAD: India's government says there are adequate fuel stocks despite shortages due to the Middle East war, but panicking citizens have triggered long queues at filling stations. India, one of the world's largest crude oil importers, relies on foreign suppliers for more than 85 per cent of its oil needs – with Russia being the biggest supplier. "Don't believe rumours," India's top oil ministry official Sujata Sharma told reporters on Wednesday.

items.title: Rising diesel costs, subsidy delays squeeze bus operators
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Stagnant cash flow, rising maintenance, and subsidy gaps are crippling the local bus industry. While the government continues to provide diesel subsidies for public transport through the Subsidised Diesel Control System (SKDS), industry leaders say the current mechanism and its scope are threatening the sustainability of many companies. Pan Malaysian Bus Operators Association (PMBOA) president Datuk Ashfar Ali said even for those who receive the subsidy, such as express bus operators, the "pay-first, refund-later" model has created a severe liquidity crisis.

items.title: Malaysia, Singapore reaffirm close coordination amid rising West Asia tensions, says Lawrence Wong
items.summary: SINGAPORE, March 26 — Singapore and Malaysia have reaffirmed their commitment to close coordination amid escalating tensions in West Asia. In a Facebook post on Thursday, Singapore Prime Minister Lawrence Wong said he and his Malaysian counterpart Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim had a constructive exchange following a phone call on the latest developments in West Asia on Wednesday. “Singapore and Malaysia will continue to coordinate closely on issues that matter to both our countries,” he said.

items.title: Sunway wins near-unanimous vote for IJM deal, gets MACC clearance
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Sunway Bhd's proposed takeover of IJM Corp Bhd has received overwhelming shareholder backing, with 99.97 per cent voting in favour at its extraordinary general meeting today. Founder and chairman Tan Sri Dr Jeffrey Cheah said the group has also received a letter from the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) clearing it of any investigation related to its acquisition of IJM shares. More to come © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: RON95 ban on foreign vehicles to be enforced from April 1
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Enforcement of a ban on the purchase of RON95 petrol by foreign-registered vehicles will take effect from April 1, Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Minister Datuk Armizan Mohd Ali said. The move expands existing regulations, which previously placed liability only on petrol station operators, to now include drivers and owners of foreign-registered vehicles. "Effective April 1, this offence will also include drivers or owners of foreign-registered vehicles," he said in a Facebook post.

items.title: Four‑month jail term for delivery rider who punched, slapped pregnant wife in Melaka
items.summary: MELAKA, March 26 — A food delivery rider was sentenced to four months in prison by the Ayer Keroh Magistrate’s Court here today after pleading guilty to two charges of causing injury to his pregnant wife. Magistrate Nur Afiqah Radhiah Zainurin handed down the sentence of four months’ jail on each count on Muhammad Aidil Azahar Rahmat, 22, and ordered the jail term to be served concurrently from the date of arrest on March 19. The man was charged with voluntarily causing hurt to his wife, Nur Azhiimah Abd Razak, 20, by punching the back of her head.

items.title: Malaysia should work with nations seeking diplomatic end to Iran war, says former minister
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Former foreign minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Abdullah has called on Malaysia to actively engage with countries pursuing diplomatic solutions to the US-Israeli war against Iran. He said that efforts must go beyond a temporary ceasefire. "Malaysia is well-placed to contribute to peace efforts," he said.

items.title: Man sentenced to four months' jail for injuring pregnant wife
items.summary: MELAKA: A man was sentenced to four months' jail for assaulting and injuring his pregnant wife. Muhammad Aidil Azahar Rahmat pleaded guilty to two counts of voluntarily causing hurt before magistrate Nur Afiqah Radhiah Zainurin. CCTV footage showed the accused punching, slapping and pulling his wife's hair.

items.title: Court awards RM6,000 to Year Two pupil injured by falling classroom fan in Selama school
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 26 — The Selama Magistrate’s Court in Perak has ordered the government and four other parties to pay RM6,000 in damages to a Year Two pupil who was injured after a ceiling fan fell on her during a classroom lesson. Magistrate R. Prabakaran made the ruling after allowing a lawsuit filed in January 2024 by the girl’s father against the school principal, the Larut Matang and Selama District Education Office, the Perak State Education Department, the Director-General of Education, and the Malaysian Government. The court found that the first to fourth defendants had failed to take reasonable steps to ensure the ceiling fan was safe and properly maintained.

items.title: MCOM Holdings offloads RM5mil liability-laden units for RM514
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: MCOM Holdings Bhd is divesting several loss-making subsidiaries with about RM5 million in net liabilities for a nominal RM514, as part of a strategic realignment. Under the first agreement, MCOM is disposing of its interests in MMT Group, comprising MCOM Messaging (Thailand) Co Ltd, Ivalent Co Ltd and Streamer Tech Co Ltd, to Chew Hwai Geeng for 1,000 Thai baht (about RM122). MMT Group is primarily involved in providing mobile payment solution services in Thailand.

items.title: RON95 ban on foreign vehicles kicks in April 1 as govt tightens fuel‑subsidy controls, says Armizan
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, March 26 — Enforcement of the ban on the purchase and sale of RON95 petrol to foreign-registered vehicles will take effect on April 1, said Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Minister Datuk Armizan Mohd Ali. He said the move was a strengthening of the existing legal framework which previously only imposed offences on petrol station operators. Armizan said the enforcement was a follow-up to the decision he presented in Parliament on Jan 29.

items.title: Ferries sail on, bus operators feel the diesel squeeze
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Ferry services in Penang will continue operating as usual despite the recent increase in diesel prices, authorities say. Penang Port Commission chairman Datuk Yeoh Soon Hin said services between the Sultan Abdul Halim Terminal (PSAH) and Raja Tun Uda Terminal (PRTU) would not be affected. However, rising diesel prices are placing increasing pressure on other transport operators.

items.title: Main-Market bound MTT Shipping upbeat on growth despite geopolitical headwinds
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: MTT Shipping and Logistics Bhd (MTTSL) remains confident in its outlook despite ongoing geopolitical pressures, underpinned by resilient cargo volumes and firm demand across its Asian service network. Managing director Ooi Lean Hin said global trade flows outside the Gulf region have stayed largely stable, helping to cushion the broader impact of disruptions. Freight rates have also seen upward pressure, particularly across routes affected by rerouting and geopolitical risk premiums.

items.title: More motorists switch to RON95 as RON97 sales fall up to 50pct
items.summary: Petrol operators said they are seeing a clear and immediate shift in consumer behaviour, with overall fuel consumption also declining. Bumiputera Petrol Station Operators Association president Datuk Abd Aziz Sapian said industry feedback indicates RON97 sales have fallen significantly, with estimates ranging from about 20 per cent to over 50 per cent depending on location and customer profile. Aziz said weekly fuel price adjustments are also contributing to increasingly volatile demand.

items.title: Police seek public help to locate 13-year-old girl reported missing in Bandar Baru Ampang
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 26 — Police are seeking public assistance to trace a 13-year-old girl, Nur Sofea Haton Abdul Hafiz, who was reported missing from her home in an apartment in Bandar Baru Ampang, Selangor, yesterday. Ampang Jaya police chief ACP Khairul Anuar Khalid said the girl was discovered missing at about 7 pm when her mother returned home, with checks finding she had left the house with a suitcase, believed to be due to a misunderstanding with her family. Those with information are urged to contact Insp Nur Alia Dyana Raimi at 017-6515201 or the Ampang Jaya district police headquarters (IPD) control centre at 03-42897222, or visit the nearest police station.

items.title: RM9.5mil paid under duress, not bribe, says Victor Chin
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Businessman Victor Chin Boon Long said a RM9.5 million payment made to the so-called "corporate mafia" group was not a bribe, but a "service fee" paid under pressure to stop ongoing harassment. Chin said the payment was not made to settle any investigation, close any case or secure any favour from enforcement agencies. "I was told that the payment of RM9.5 million would be passed to the corporate mafia group to make them happy, stop the hostilities against my people, and give me a second chance to live," he said in a statement today.

items.title: Yeoh orders probe into 'protection money' video in Wangsa Maju
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Federal Territories Minister Hannah Yeoh has directed that a police report be lodged over a viral video showing two men allegedly demanding "protection money" from a massage parlour in Wangsa Maju. @umno_batu Salam, *SOP Baru MPP????? * Seawal pagi ini, ada beberapa kawan menghantar clip video yang menunjukkan 2 orang wakil Ahli Majlis Perwakilan Penduduk (MPP) Wangsa Maju hadir dalam keadaan mencurigakan ke sebuah rumah urut mungkin (bersikap husnuzon) untuk menerangkan SOP baru MPP.

items.title: Water levels fall below normal across Kelantan amid prolonged hot weather
items.summary: KOTA BARU: Water levels across Kelantan have fallen below normal following prolonged hot weather, affecting dams and rivers statewide. Data from Public Info Banjir also showed several major rivers below normal levels, including Sungai Golok in Rantau Panjang at 3.23m compared with its normal 5m. Sungai Golok in Kampung Jenob, Tanah Merah, recorded 18.23m, below the normal 19m, while Sungai Kelantan in Kuala Krai stood at 15.63m compared with its normal 17m.

items.title: Gruesome discovery of three family members dead in Sungai Petani
items.summary: SUNGAI PETANI: Police discovered three family members dead in a house in Kampung Belau, Merbok, after a foul stench was reported. Kuala Muda police chief Assistant Commissioner Hanyan Ramlan said police were alerted by the public at around 12.30am, reporting that the couple had not been seen for three days and a strong odour was coming from the house. Hanyan said initial investigations point to a murder case, with the suspect also believed to be dead.

items.title: EU experts want broad restrictions on 'forever chemicals'
items.summary: HELSINKI: "Forever chemicals" pose a growing risk to health and the environment and should be broadly banned across the European Union, with some exceptions, EU experts said in findings published on Thursday. "PFAS pose growing risks to people and the environment. The first committee, the Risk Assessment Committee (RAC), found "that PFAS pose growing risks to people and the environment."

items.title: Firefighters battle blaze at recyclable materials site in Shah Alam
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: A fire broke out at a pile of recyclable materials in an open area in Section 36 here, sending thick black smoke billowing into the sky. Selangor Fire and Rescue Department assistant director of operations Ahmad Mukhlis Mukhtar said the department is currently battling the fire after receiving an emergency call at 1.24pm. "The operations commander reported that the fire involved a pile of recyclable materials in an open area measuring approximately two acres (0.809ha).

items.title: MIC stays with BN, cites stability, multiracial strength
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Indian Congress (MIC) decision to remain with Barisan Nasional (BN) reflects a firm, forward-looking stance and the coalition's multiracial strength. BN secretary-general Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abd Kadir said the move is rooted in a long-standing history, from the Alliance era (1955–1973) to BN's formation in 1974. "People need a stable, experienced platform like BN, which has proven its ability to govern inclusively and pragmatically.

items.title: Ex-Taipei mayor jailed 17 years for corruption
items.summary: TAIPEI: A former Taipei City mayor who once ran for the presidency was sentenced on Thursday to 17 years in prison for taking bribes and misusing political donations. Ko Wen-je, the founder of the Taiwan People's Party (TPP), which has become a third force in Taiwanese politics, was found guilty by the Taipei District Court where dozens of supporters had gathered ahead of the verdict. Ko, 66, was charged in December 2024 with corruption relating to a property development when he was Taipei's mayor.

items.title: Girl, 12, wins RM6,000 over classroom fan accident
items.summary: Selama magistrate Prabakaran Rajoo ordered the government and four others to pay the girl RM6,000 for pain and suffering, plus RM1,075 in costs. She named the school's headmaster, the Larut Matang and Selama district education officer, the Perak state education director, the education director-general, and the government as the first to fifth defendants. She said the incident was caused by negligence, with the school and officials failing to maintain the ceiling fan and ensure pupil safety.

items.title: Builders call for govt support amid diesel price hike
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Master Builders Association Malaysia (MBAM) has called for urgent government support and collective action amid escalating cost pressures following the recent diesel price hike. Its president Oliver HC Wee said the construction industry is grappling with a convergence of challenges driven by geopolitical tensions in the Middle East, which have disrupted global supply chains and driven up material and energy costs. Wee said the industry is seeking a series of targeted and timely measures from the government to ensure continuity and stability.

items.title: Israel medics say Iranian missile attack leaves 6 wounded
items.summary: TEL AVIV: The Israeli military said air defences responded to missile attacks from Iran on Thursday that according to medics left six people lightly wounded and caused some damage. The military said Home Front Command rescuers were called to an impact site "in central Israel." Israeli media said six rockets were intercepted.

items.title: Water supply in north stable for now, expert warns of rising risks
items.summary: Climate disaster and water resources management expert Dr Chong Khai Lin said while Malaysia is not facing water bankruptcy at the national level, the situation in the north points to rising regional risk. Chong, who is director of the Disaster Management Institute at Universiti Utara Malaysia, said Kedah is currently experiencing clear water stress driven by extended hot and dry conditions, compounded by sustained demand from agriculture, domestic use and industry. Chong said the critically low levels at Muda Dam are a key concern, but stressed that the situation must be viewed within the broader Muda Agricultural Development Authority (Mada) system.

items.title: Viral video of alleged solicitation at Wangsa Maju massage centre sparks police probe
items.summary: PETALING JAYA, March 26 — Authorities have launched an investigation after a video showing two men allegedly soliciting bribes from a massage centre in Wangsa Maju, Kuala Lumpur, went viral. Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh, in a statement on her Facebook page said a police report had been filed. She said she had also instructed and that Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) to investigate the matter immediately.

items.title: North Korea, Belarus sign 'friendship' treaty during Lukashenko visit
items.summary: SEOUL: North Korea and Belarus's strongmen leaders signed a "friendship and cooperation" treaty on Thursday after Kim Jong Un gave a lavish welcome to President Alexander Lukashenko on his maiden visit. Besides supporting Russia's war against Ukraine – around 2,000 North Korean soldiers are thought to have died – both nations are under Western sanctions and are accused of gross human rights violations. Lukashenko visited the Kumsusan Palace of the Sun – where Kim's embalmed father and grandfather lie in state – to pay his respects, flanked by top North Korean officials, the Korean Central News Agency reported.

items.title: King tells public to take extreme caution amid heatwave
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, has advised the public to exercise extreme caution and take necessary health precautions as the nation continues to grapple with a prolonged heatwave. In a posting via his official Facebook page today, His Majesty emphasised the importance of staying hydrated and minimising outdoor exposure to mitigate health risks associated with high temperatures. Expressing concern for the younger generation, Sultan Ibrahim called on the Education Ministry to take proactive measures to safeguard the well-being of students.

items.title: Caretakers in fireworks stall fire case released on police bail, probe ongoing [WATCH]
items.summary: NIBONG TEBAL: Three caretakers, including a woman, detained to assist in investigations into a fireworks stall fire in Taman Pekaka have been released on police bail. "They have been released on police bail. They were detained following a fire that destroyed a fireworks stall in Taman Pekaka.

items.title: Hormuz gambit - How Teheran rewrote the rules of geopolitical power
items.summary: FOR decades, Western strategists maintained a quiet consensus: Iran could threaten to close the Strait of Hormuz, but it could never truly do so. The United States Navy stood ready. The "rules-based international order" would hold.

items.title: Statelessness affects all Malaysians, not just ethnic Indians or Chinese, says Selangor Speaker
items.summary: PETALING JAYA, March 26 — Being stateless or not having any citizenship is not just a problem that affects the Malaysian Indian or Malaysian Chinese communities, but all actually experience it and should therefore not be racialised by politicians, Selangor Speaker Lau Weng San has said. “The issue of statelessness is not only an Indian or Chinese or non-Malay or non-Muslim issue,” he said in his speech at non-governmental organisation DHRRA Malaysia’s 20th anniversary dinner. Lau said this was based on his own experience when he accompanied one of the stateless persons who was going to the National Registration Department (NRD) for interviews as part of the application for Malaysian citizenship.

items.title: Newborn found in bin in Ampang; teen among five remanded in Melaka baby death probe
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 26 — A newborn baby girl was discovered in a rubbish bin outside a house in Taman Putra, Ampang, yesterday. Ampang Jaya District Police chief ACP Khairul Anuar Khalid said police received a report from a local man, who is also the occupant of the house, at 6.27 pm. “The baby still had her umbilical cord attached.

items.title: Firefighters respond to mercury spill at Penang dialysis centre
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: A broken blood pressure machine caused a mercury spill at the Buddhist Tzu Chi Dialysis Centre in Penang, prompting a fire department response. Penang Fire and Rescue Department assistant operations director John Sagun Francis said the incident involved a blood pressure machine that had broken, causing a mercury spill. "Firefighters wore protective gear before cleaning up the spill using sulphur powder," he said in a statement.

items.title: Critical alert as Muda Dam plunges to 8.04pc, threatening upcoming planting season
items.summary: ALOR SETAR, March 26 — The water level at the Muda Dam in Sik has dropped to a worrying low of just 8.04 per cent, or 10,050 acre-feet, as of today. Muda Agricultural Development Authority (MADA) chairman Datuk Dr Ismail Salleh said the dam plays a crucial role in collecting water before channelling it to Pedu Dam for irrigation across the Muda agricultural area. Combined levels at Muda, Pedu and Ahning dams stand at about 39 per cent.

items.title: Anwar: Malaysia, China to strengthen diplomatic and security relations
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia and China have pledged to further strengthen diplomatic relations and security cooperation to safeguard the social and economic prosperity enjoyed by both nations amid current and future global challenges. Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the matter was discussed during a courtesy call by China's Minister of State Security Chen Yixin, who is on his first visit to China's good-friend country, Malaysia, this year. The prime minister also expressed hope that the progress achieved jointly across various sectors would continue to drive closer ties between Malaysia and China.

items.title: SCA Solutions plans ACE Market listing to fund Kapar facility
items.summary: The group, which provides heating, ventilation and air conditioning (HVAC) and fire safety instrumentation solutions, plans to issue 114 million new ordinary shares. Another 71.2 million shares will be placed out to Bumiputera investors approved by the Investment, Trade and Industry Ministry, with 8.6 million shares allocated to selected investors via private placement. SCA Solutions specialises in HVAC, fire safety and water treatment systems for buildings.

items.title: PM: Malaysia, China renew pledge to deepen diplomatic and security cooperation amid global tensions
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 26 — Malaysia and China have pledged to further strengthen diplomatic relations and security cooperation to safeguard the social and economic prosperity enjoyed by both nations amid current and future global challenges. Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the matter was discussed during a courtesy call by China’s Minister of State Security Chen Yixin, who is on his first visit to China’s good-friend country, Malaysia, this year. The Prime Minister also expressed hope that the progress achieved jointly across various sectors would continue to drive closer ties between Malaysia and China.

items.title: Kedah police: Foul odour leads to discovery of three bodies in Merbok home
items.summary: SUNGAI PETANI, March 27 — A foul smell emanating from a house in Kampung Belau, Merbok, near here, led to the discovery of the bodies of three family members late last night. Kuala Muda police chief Hanyan Ramlan said the victims were identified as Zikri Abdul Rahman, 65, his wife Suraya Man, 53, and her son, Muhammad Shakir Zufayri Abdul Aziz, 29. Police also recovered several sharp weapons from the house.

items.title: Eight men arrested for allegedly assaulting police officers during George Town chase incident
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, March 26 — Police have detained eight men in connection with an incident last night at an apartment complex on Jalan Sungai, where a group of suspects allegedly assaulted police officers following a misunderstanding. Penang police chief Datuk Azizee Ismail said all eight men, aged 28 to 39, were arrested separately in the Northeast district after the incident. Meanwhile, Northeast District police chief ACP Abdul Rozak Muhammad said the district control centre received a call for help from officers on duty at 9.50 pm yesterday, following problems before the incident.

items.title: BN sec-gen says MIC’s decision to stay in coalition reflects loyalty, stability and multiracial cooperation
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 26 — Barisan Nasional secretary-general Datuk Seri Zambry Abdul Kadir said MIC’s decision to remain in the coalition is a reflection of the party’s loyalty, political maturity, and commitment to national stability. Zambry congratulated the party’s Central Working Committee (CWC) for the “clear, firm and forward-looking” decision, noting that BN has endured multiple political and racial challenges since the Perikatan era (1955–1973) and through the formation of BN from 1974 to the present. He said several factors, including the need for stability and continuity amid a fragmented political landscape, guided the decision.

items.title: Langkawi ferry services cut to three daily trips as diesel costs jump over 100pc
items.summary: Ferry Line Ventures Sdn Bhd (Ferry Line) general manager Dr Baharin Baharom said his party had no choice but to reduce trips because they were concerned that the ferry transport industry to Langkawi was facing the risk of going out of business following the sudden spike in industrial diesel prices. He said the increase in diesel prices of more than 100 per cent was putting great pressure on operating costs and ferry operators in Langkawi and the steps taken were necessary to ensure the continuity of the company’s operations. Schedule rearrangement of trips is necessary to reduce the risk of losses that are increasingly burdening ferry operators.

items.title: Police bust four motorcycle theft syndicates in Klang Valley, 21 arrested, 24 bikes seized
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 26 — Police have crippled four active vehicle theft syndicates in the capital following the arrest of 21 suspects and the seizure of 24 motorcycles worth an estimated RM248,000 in operations conducted from Jan 1 until last Friday. Wangsa Maju police chief SAC Mohamad Lazim Ismail said the suspects, aged between 33 and 50, and comprising 20 men and a woman, were detained in the Klang Valley area. “They exploit the carelessness of owners who leave keys on their motorcycles, particularly in hotspot areas like People’s Housing Projects (PPR) and commercial centres around Setapak and Wangsa Maju,” he told reporters after an event at the Wangsa Maju District Police headquarters here today.

items.title: Military confirms missing Op Pasir soldier was AWOL after vanishing in Sabah, found in Sarawak and now in custody
items.summary: The Joint Force Headquarters (MK AB) said Ranger Fredenick Petrus, 21, from Company C of the 6th Battalion, Royal Ranger Regiment (6 RRD), was discovered missing during a morning roll call at the Pulau Pandanan Post on March 14, prompting immediate tracking efforts. According to the statement, the soldier was later arrested on March 19 by the 3rd Company of the Royal Military Police Corps (KPTD) in Kota Samarahan, Sarawak. “He was subsequently brought before the Kota Samarahan Magistrate and is currently being held in military custody at Muara Tuang Camp, Kota Samarahan, for further investigation,” read the statement.

items.title: Victor Chin says RM9.5m payment to corporate mafia was a service fee to protect associates
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 26 — Businessman Victor Chin Boon Long has clarified that a RM9.5 million payment made to the so-called Corporate Mafia gang was not a bribe, but a “service fee” paid under pressure to stop ongoing harassment. In a statement today, Chin said the payment was not made to influence enforcement agencies, close any investigation, or settle allegations of wrongdoing against him. “I was told that the payment of RM9.5 million would be passed to the Corporate Mafia gang to make them happy, stop the hostilities against my people, and also give me a second chance to live,” Chin said.

items.title: Ewon: Sabah to table Kota Belud Industrial Park Master Plan at next Cabinet meeting
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, March 26 — The Kota Belud Industrial Park Master Plan will be tabled at the upcoming Sabah State Cabinet meeting, said Deputy Chief Minister III Datuk Ewon Benedick. Ewon, who is also the Minister of Industrial Development, Entrepreneurship and Transport, said the tabling of the cabinet paper was in line with the state government’s previous announcement to develop new industrial parks in Kota Belud and Kudat as an effort to strengthen economic growth in the state. He said the implementation of the Kota Belud Industrial Park development would be handled by Kota Kinabalu Industrial Park (KKIP), an agency under his ministry.

items.title: Selangor Speaker: Federal govt should ‘seriously consider’ DHRRA’s five proposals to aid Malaysia’s stateless community
items.summary: PETALING JAYA, March 26 — Malaysia’s federal government should look into non-governmental organisation DHRRA Malaysia’s five proposals to close legal loopholes and help the country’s stateless community, including those who have at least one Malaysian parent, the Selangor Speaker has said. Selangor Speaker Lau Weng San said the Selangor state government could also look into how it could help the stateless community, if DHRRA Malaysia brings these proposals to the Selangor state legislative assembly. “I think it should be given serious consideration by the government, especially the federal government.

items.title: Couple jailed one year for indecent act at Chinese cemetery in Penang
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, March 26 — A couple arrested for committing an indecent act at a Chinese cemetery in Penang was sentenced to one year’s jail each by the Magistrate’s Court in George Town, according to New Straits Times (NST). According to the report, Magistrate Nadratun Naim Mohd Saidi handed down the sentence on M. Jegathesan, 58, and Halila Abu Bakar, 37, after they pleaded guilty to the offence under Section 377D of the Penal Code. Halila Abu Bakar, 37, is escorted by police after being sentenced to 12 months’ jail by the Magistrate’s Court.

items.title: King warns of health and fire risks, calls for precautions as Malaysia faces hot spell
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 26 — His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, the King of Malaysia, has urged the Ministry of Education (MOE) to take serious measures to protect the health of students as the country grapples with a prolonged hot weather phenomenon. “His Majesty emphasised the importance of maintaining health by reducing outdoor activities that are exposed to the hot weather, as well as ensuring adequate water intake,” the message read. A special advisory was also issued for vulnerable groups, with the King advising that the elderly and children suffering from chronic illnesses not be exposed to the hot weather in order to avoid any complications.

items.title: Two men remanded four days in Johor Bahru over blaze that destroyed fireworks tent
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, March 26 — Two men, aged 30 and 35, have been remanded for four days starting today to assist in the investigation into a fire at a pop‑up tent selling fireworks along Jalan Bunga Ros in Kampung Dato’ Sulaiman Menteri here last Monday. Magistrate Chai Ing Hien granted the remand for the two suspects after receiving an application from the police at the Magistrate’s Court here this morning. Following the incident, police arrested two male suspects from a residential area in Kulai and the Kampung Melayu Majidee area here yesterday.

items.title: Southkey murder probe widens as sixth suspect remanded, four others’ remand extended in Johor
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, March 26 — A sixth suspect has been remanded for seven days starting today to assist in the murder probe of a foreigner who was killed in Southkey here on March 14. The same court also granted a one‑week remand extension until April 1 for four other suspects, including a woman, after their initial remand period ended today. Previously, the same court also granted an extension of the remand period for another suspect, which ended yesterday, until March 31.

items.title: Malaysia to ramp up domestic drug production support under NIMP 2030, BMI reports
items.summary: In a statement today, the Fitch Solutions research unit said Malaysia aims to increase the share of domestically-produced medical products under NIMP 2030, creating a more supportive policy backdrop for the local manufacturing of essential medicines, including generic medicines. “Together, these measures will accelerate the shift in public procurement toward locally produced, cost-effective generic medicines,” it said. Beyond lowering drug expenditure, the policy is designed to reshape clinicians’ prescribing habits and strengthen public confidence in generic medicines, said BMI.

items.title: Hannah Yeoh’s first 100 days: 13 reforms to strengthen governance and transparency in the Federal Territories
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 26 — Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh has introduced 13 reform measures within her first 100 days in office to strengthen governance and increase administrative transparency in the capital. In a Facebook post today, she said the initiatives focus on improving administration and service delivery, including tackling tender cartels, widening public access to systems, and reinforcing checks and balances. She also conveyed appreciation to Kuala Lumpur Mayor Datuk Seri Fadlun Mak Ujud, former Director-General Datuk Seri Noridah Abdul Rahim, Director Datuk Muhammad Azmi Mohd Zain, and the leadership of the Federal Territories Department for their support.

items.title: Malaysia, Pakistan foreign ministers urge de‑escalation, stress diplomacy in West Asia conflict
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 26 — Malaysian Foreign Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan and Pakistani Deputy Prime Minister and Foreign Minister Mohammad Ishaq Dar have underscored the need for de-escalation through dialogue and diplomacy amid the war in West Asia. In a post on X, Pakistan’s Foreign Ministry said Dar and Mohamad discussed the war and issues of mutual interest during a telephone conversation. “The two leaders discussed recent regional and international developments of mutual interest and emphasised the importance of de-escalation, dialogue, and diplomacy to address ongoing challenges,” it said.

items.title: ETS, KTM Komuter services on Batu Caves–Pulau Sebang route fully restored
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 26 — Keretapi Tanah Melayu Berhad has announced that the Electric Train Service (ETS) and KTM Komuter operations on the Batu Caves-Pulau Sebang-Batu Caves route have resumed fully following earlier disruptions caused by damage to overhead line equipment (ATA). In a statement today, KTMB said repair works on the electrification system at KM 434.423 between Batang Benar and Labu stations were completed at 3.56 am today. KTMB confirmed that all affected ETS and KTM Komuter passengers have since safely reached their respective destinations.

items.title: PLUS urges Raya travellers to plan return trips as 2.3 million vehicles expected on highways
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 26 — PLUS Malaysia Berhad has urged motorists to plan their return journeys to the Klang Valley using its MyPLUS-TTA digital travel schedule amid an expected surge in post-Hari Raya traffic. “Planning return journeys according to these recommended travel times will significantly assist in alleviating traffic congestion along the highway,” it said. PLUS said it will activate Smart Lanes at 34 strategic locations and deploy PLUSRonda teams for 24-hour patrols to assist motorists.

items.title: Anwar expresses support for Qatar, condemns attack on Ras Laffan LNG facility
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 26 — Malaysia has firmly expressed its support for Qatar’s sovereignty and strongly condemned the attack targeting the Ras Laffan Liquefied Natural Gas (LNG) facility, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said. Anwar said that in a telephone conversation with Qatar’s Prime Minister Sheikh Mohammed bin Abdulrahman Al Thani, Malaysia described the energy infrastructure as critical to the global economy. a Anwar said that he and Sheikh Mohammed also exchanged Aidilfitri greetings and expressed concern over the worsening security situation in West Asia.

items.title: Police detain man over viral Facebook post insulting Prophet Muhammad
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 26 — Police detained a 47-year-old local man suspected of insulting Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) over a Facebook post that recently went viral. Kuala Lumpur police chief Datuk Fadil Marsus said a report was received at 1.30 am last Sunday regarding the offensive post uploaded by an account using the name ‘Sakti Sakti Sakti’. He said the suspect was arrested by a police team at 8.32 pm yesterday in Butterworth, Penang.
